canvas-mist.$description: Canvas Mist — Page background, product photography backdrop, the dominant surface — everything else sits on this almost-white field

ink-black.$description: Ink Black — Primary body text, dominant hairline borders, footer rules — the most-used color in the system at ~1017 occurrences, functioning as the typographic anchor

carbon.$description: Carbon — Near-black with a barely-perceptible violet cast — used for dark hero bands, inverted text, and footer/divider blocks where pure black would feel too clinical

steel-gray.$description: Steel Gray — Light border hairlines, nav icon fills, list dividers — the structural gray that organizes the page into ruled sections without ever calling attention to itself

graphite.$description: Graphite — Muted secondary borders and subdued helper text — the quietest neutral, used only when a rule needs to be present but not assertive

te-20.$description: The signature voice — a custom industrial sans used for nearly everything. Weight 100 (ultra-thin) at 13px is the body default and it is an anti-convention: most sites use 400-500 for body copy, this one uses a hairline that lets the photography do the talking. Weight 300 takes over at 19px and up for nav items, labels, and mid-size UI. Substitute: Inter at weight 100, or Suisse Int'l Thin — both preserve the wire-like quality that makes teenage engineering's type feel engineered rather than designed

te-40.$description: Reserved for primary page headings at exactly 40px in light weight 300 — the lightest possible headline voice paired with tight 1.11 leading keeps the type block compact and poster-like against the generous canvas

canvas-mist.$description: Surface level 1: Page background, default product photography backdrop — the base plane that almost everything sits on

pure-white-implicit.$description: Surface level 2: Product image cards appear on white within the canvas — the photographic bleed creates an even brighter island inside the near-white field

carbon.$description: Surface level 3: Dark hero bands and footer — the only elevation device in the system, used to frame products like display cases
